title: Pin the 40GB LTX transformer resident across the stage-1 -> stage-2 transition (kill the per-stage re-fault)
description:
The single biggest remaining LTX perf lever, root-caused but never shipped. aimdo EVICTS the 40GB LTXAV transformer between ComfyUI subgraphs, so stage-2 (continue/upscale) re-faults the ENTIRE 40GB from scratch even though stage-1 had it resident seconds earlier. ~60-93s of every multi-stage gen is reloading an already-loaded model; the real stage-2 sampler is ~5s.

=== LIVE EVIDENCE (2026-07-04, cu130 Pod, RTX 4090 24GB, bf16 after MPI-190 revert) ===
- t2v: 93s between stage-1 and stage-2 (pure re-fault).
- i2v warm full: 1m54s; ~60s of it is the stage transition, ~54s real work.
- preview (stage-1): 30s first-latent fault + ~20s remaining 7 steps = ~1m02s.
- preview -> continue (stage-2): ~60s to START inference, then inference ~5s. So ~92% of the continue is re-fault, ~8% is compute.
These are POST-cu130 numbers: cu130 already cut the per-stage re-fault from ~34-58s to ~12-26s baseline, but at these resolutions/tiers it's still 60-93s. bf16 and GGUF pay this IDENTICALLY (it's aimdo eviction, not the transformer format) so MPI-190's revert neither caused nor fixed it.

=== THE FIX (from docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md, Fix #2 / line ~93-96, 374 — untested lever) ===
Make aimdo/DynamicVRAM keep the 40GB LTXAV transformer resident across the stage-1 -> stage-2 boundary instead of evicting between subgraphs. On a card where the 40GB fits with room (32GB+), or where dynamic-VRAM can pin it, the second fault never fires -> continue drops from ~60-93s to ~5-11s.
OPEN QUESTIONS to investigate (do NOT single-symptom patch — cf research-first-on-version-breaks):
1. WHY does aimdo evict between ComfyUI subgraphs when the model was just resident? Is it a comfy-aimdo cache policy, a --cache-* flag, or a DynamicVRAM setting?
2. Node 58 (MpiClearVram) is NOT the cause (removing it made stage-2 WORSE: 69 s/it vs 34 s/it — it de-fragments so the re-fault lands clean; see pod-perf doc). So the fix is NOT 'remove node 58'.
3. Is there an aimdo 'keep-resident' / 'no-evict-between-prompts' knob? (--cache-none is the OPPOSITE — do not use.)
4. Does the two-prompt (preview then continue) structure force the eviction because they're SEPARATE ComfyUI prompts? If so, would a single-prompt gate (Input_Is_Continue already baked in) that never leaves the graph avoid the re-fault?

=== HOT LEAD: LOCAL ENGINE (2.12+cu130, driver 595) HALVES THE GAP vs POD (2.10+cu130, driver 580) — 2026-07-04 ===
Measured the app's LOCAL engine (engine/ComfyUI_windows_portable/python_embeded) after the user updated the workflow + ran a WARM gen on a 4060 Ti (Ada sm_89, SAME arch as the Pod 4090):
  LOCAL:  torch 2.12.0+cu130, CUDA-built 13.0, cuDNN 9.2.0, driver 595.97 (runtime CUDA 13.2), 4060 Ti sm_89 -> warm 129s, stage1->stage2 gap <30s.
  POD:    torch 2.10.0+cu130, CUDA-built 13.0, driver 580.x, 4090 sm_89          -> warm ~114s (1m54s), stage gap 60-93s.
The local stage-gap is ~HALF the Pod's on the SAME GPU arch. The uncontrolled variables between them: torch minor (2.12 vs 2.10, BOTH cu130) and driver (595 vs 580). BOTH cu130 (the MPI-187 lever is held constant), so this is a NEW axis MPI-187 never tested — it only A/B'd 2.11+cu126 vs 2.10+cu130, never two cu130 builds at different torch minors. HYPOTHESIS: torch 2.12+cu130 (newer SDPA kernel / memory-reuse / fault path) and/or the newer driver 595 shrinks the inter-stage re-fault. CHEAP TESTABLE LEVERS on the Pod (in order):
  (a) bump Pod torch 2.10.0+cu130 -> 2.12.0+cu130 (match local), keep everything else. Re-measure the stage gap.
  (b) bump the Pod host driver floor (already r580; local is 595) — newer UVM/fault-hook path.
WIN target: get the Pod stage-gap down to the local <30s. NOTE this may be independent of / complementary to Fix #2 (keep-resident) — test the torch bump FIRST (one Dockerfile line + rebuild) before the deeper aimdo-config work, since it's the cheapest lever with a proven local baseline. Caution (feedback [[research-first-on-version-breaks]]): a torch bump can break aimdo 0.4.10 / node compat — verify a full gen still runs, don't just chase the timing.

=== PRODUCT CONTEXT ===
The multi-stage preview->continue split is a deliberate FEATURE (iterate-cheap-commit-once, user's 2026-06-28 call) — the ~30s preview is fine. This card targets ONLY the wasteful 60-93s re-fault on the CONTINUE, not the staged structure itself.

=== SCOPE ===
Likely lives in: the ComfyUI workflow structure (comfy_workflows/scripts/workflow_generation/generate_ltx.py + the template's stage gating), aimdo config/flags on the Pod (mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/start-*.sh or the wrapper), or DynamicVRAM settings. All live Pod ops are USER-only. Read the pod-perf-investigation.md doc FIRST (RESOLVED banner + Fix #2 section) before touching anything.

BRANCH: RunPod (+ possibly mpi-ci for aimdo config). Cross-ref: MPI-190 (bf16 revert that surfaced these clean numbers), MPI-187 (cu130 that cut the baseline), docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md (root cause + the untested Fix #2), MPI-157 (node 58 / MpiClearVram — keep it).
